reasoning: The informal statement asks: if P is a normal Sylow p-subgroup of G, then every automorphism φ of G satisfies φ(P) = P. In Mathlib, `Subgroup.Characteristic H` is defined precisely as `∀ ϕ : G ≃* G, H.comap ϕ.toMonoidHom = H`, which is equivalent to "φ(P) = P for every automorphism φ of G" (via `characteristic_iff_map_eq`). The hypotheses match: G is an arbitrary group, p is prime (`hp : Nat.Prime p`), P is a Sylow p-subgroup (`P : Sylow p G`), and P is normal (`hP : P.Normal`). No finiteness assumption on G is silently added or dropped; the informal statement also doesn't require finiteness, and the result actually holds in general (if P is a normal Sylow p-subgroup, then for any other Sylow p-subgroup Q, PQ is a p-subgroup containing P, hence equals P by maximality, so P is unique and thus fixed by every automorphism). The quantifier structure and conclusion match exactly.
